failure_chain: kgsl_pipe_new() calls IOCTL_KGSL_DRAWCTXT_CREATE — FAILS with EINVAL

root_cause.flags_tested: ALL single bits 0-31, all combinations — ALL fail
root_cause.paradox: Turnip Vulkan successfully calls the SAME ioctl (same flags 0x23, same fd) during vkCreateDevice and it WORKS
root_cause.hypothesis_1: Kernel-level ioctl filtering (SELinux ioctl_xperm or seccomp) that allows ioctl from specific library code paths but not from arbitrary userspace
root_cause.hypothesis_2: KGSL kernel driver internal state machine requires specific initialization sequence before DRAWCTXT_CREATE that Turnip performs implicitly through Vulkan loader path
root_cause.hypothesis_3: The Vulkan loader (libvulkan.so from /system/lib64) performs a domain transition or capability grant that enables the ioctl

turnip_proof.same_fd_after_vkCreateDevice: Our DRAWCTXT_CREATE still FAILS on Turnip's fd

conclusion.explanation: KGSL kernel driver on this Android 14 device (Adreno 730v3) rejects IOCTL_KGSL_DRAWCTXT_CREATE from direct userspace calls. Turnip succeeds because it goes through the system Vulkan loader which may have special kernel-level permissions or initialization. The freedreno Gallium path cannot create a GPU context without this ioctl.
conclusion.rusticl_role: research_only — confirmed blocked at kernel ioctl level
next_steps: Investigate if system Vulkan loader performs seccomp/SELinux transition
Check if running from a rooted shell (su) allows the ioctl
Consider using Turnip Vulkan compute as bridge to Rusticl (not practical)
